what: PheWAS — one variant across all traits
title: Query benchmarks
text: PheWAS reads one row z-score matrix (one variant × every trait). database 9 trait chunks, minimum many compressed chunks must decompressed per query.
code: t_phewas <- elapsed(pw <- phewas(db, "19:45412079_C_T")) cat(sprintf("PheWAS single variant:  %.1f s  (%d associations returned)\n",             t_phewas, nrow(pw))) #> PheWAS single variant:  2.3 s  (3382 associations returned)

title: Query benchmarks
text: region PheWAS iterates every variant window. Time scales roughly linearly number matched variants additional variant adds one set trait-chunk reads (z-score Neff).
code: # 100 kb window centred on the FTO peak (chr16 ~53.8 Mb) n_fto <- sum(   db$variants$chrom == "16" &   db$variants$pos   >= 53.75e6 &   db$variants$pos   <= 53.85e6 ) cat("Variants in FTO 100 kb window:", n_fto, "\n") #> Variants in FTO 100 kb window: 6  t_fto <- elapsed(fto <- phewas(db, "16:53.75e6-53.85e6")) cat(sprintf("PheWAS 100 kb region:   %.1f s  (%d associations, %d variants)\n",             t_fto, nrow(fto), n_fto)) #> PheWAS 100 kb region:   7.9 s  (19493 associations, 6 variants)

what: GWAS — all variants for one trait
title: Query benchmarks
text: GWAS reads one column z-score matrix (every variant × one trait). database 187 variant chunks.
code: t_gwas <- elapsed(bmi <- gwas(db, "ukb-b-19953")) cat(sprintf("GWAS single trait:      %.1f s  (%d associations returned)\n",             t_gwas, nrow(bmi))) #> GWAS single trait:      4.1 s  (67556 associations returned)

title: Query benchmarks
text: tophits() uses pre-computed sparse COO index two standard thresholds (5e-8 1e-5), significant pairs read rather scanning full matrix.
code: # Single trait, genome-wide significance — uses 5e-8 mask t_th1 <- elapsed(th1 <- tophits(db, traits = "ukb-b-19953", pval = 5e-8)) cat(sprintf("tophits 1 trait  (mask, p<5e-8):  %.2f s  (%d hits)\n",             t_th1, nrow(th1))) #> tophits 1 trait  (mask, p<5e-8):  2.32 s  (16 hits)  # Five traits, suggestive threshold — uses 1e-5 mask five_traits <- c("ukb-b-19953", "ebi-a-GCST006867",                  "ebi-a-GCST90018961", "ebi-a-GCST003116",                  "ebi-a-GCST90002412") t_th5 <- elapsed(th5 <- tophits(db, traits = five_traits, pval = 1e-5)) cat(sprintf("tophits 5 traits (mask, p<1e-5):  %.2f s  (%d hits)\n",             t_th5, nrow(th5))) #> tophits 5 traits (mask, p<1e-5):  4.98 s  (81 hits)

title: Query benchmarks
text: non-standard p-value threshold requested mask exist, function falls back scanning trait column full — much slower.
code: t_scan <- elapsed(th_scan <- tophits(db, traits = "ukb-b-19953", pval = 1e-6)) cat(sprintf("tophits 1 trait (scan,  p<1e-6):  %.1f s  (%d hits)\n",             t_scan, nrow(th_scan))) #> tophits 1 trait (scan,  p<1e-6):  2.7 s  (24 hits)

title: Query benchmarks
text: associations() reads individual cells; time scales number unique (variant-chunk, trait-chunk) intersections need read.
code: # Three variants near LDL-pathway genes present in this database ldl_vars <- c(   "19:45412079_C_T",  # APOE region   "1:55014160_C_T",   # PCSK9 region   "2:21870368_A_G"    # APOB region ) two_traits <- c("ebi-a-GCST90018961", "ebi-a-GCST003116")  t_3x2 <- elapsed(a1 <- associations(db, variants = ldl_vars, traits = two_traits)) cat(sprintf("associations  3 variants × 2 traits:  %.3f s  (%d rows)\n",             t_3x2, nrow(a1))) #> associations  3 variants × 2 traits:  1.291 s  (5 rows) # 10 random variants × 5 traits set.seed(42) vars_10  <- sample(db$variants$alid, 10) traits_5 <- c("ukb-b-19953", "ebi-a-GCST006867",               "ebi-a-GCST90018961", "ebi-a-GCST003116",               "ebi-a-GCST90002412")  t_10x5 <- elapsed(a2 <- associations(db, variants = vars_10, traits = traits_5)) cat(sprintf("associations 10 variants × 5 traits:  %.3f s  (%d rows)\n",             t_10x5, nrow(a2))) #> associations 10 variants × 5 traits:  1.758 s  (41 rows)

title: Query benchmarks
text: .pleiodb chunk 512 × 512 zstd-compressed block. pleiodbr groups query pairs natural chunk boundaries decompresses unique chunk per query, mirroring approach used Python pleiodb library. number chunk decompressions therefore equals number distinct (v-chunk, t-chunk) pairs touched: PheWAS (1 variant, traits) decompresses 9 trait chunks — one per trait chunk, z-scores Neff. GWAS (1 trait, variants) decompresses 187 variant chunks. tophits query pre-built mask reads chunks contain least one significant pair — usually small fraction full matrix. mask filter now also applies non-standard thresholds: e.g. pval = 1e-6 reads 1e-5 mask (superset) filters, avoiding full column scan. remaining gap relative Python per-element decode overhead: Python decodes int16 arrays NumPy’s frombuffer() (zero-copy view buffer, C-level arithmetic), R uses readBin() followed .double() element-wise arithmetic — 3–4 ms per 512×512 chunk vs. ~0.5 ms Python. function call R also carries R-interpreter overhead NumPy’s C loops avoid entirely.

title: Query benchmarks
text: pleiodb Python library implements queries NumPy, achieving roughly order magnitude lower latency R database. Equivalent Python code two core queries: Timings recorded server (main.pleiodb, 95 k variants × 4,159 traits): implementations make number chunk reads decompressions. residual R overhead per-element decode cost: readBin() + .double() + arithmetic allocates two R vectors iterates R interpreter, whereas NumPy’s frombuffer() zero-copy view followed vectorised C arithmetic. workflows sub-second PheWAS latency critical, call Python library R via reticulate.
code: import sys, time sys.path.insert(0, "/path/to/pleiodb/src") from pleiodb.db import GWASDatabase  db = GWASDatabase.open("/local-scratch/data/pleiodb/main.pleiodb") v  = db.variant_index(["19:45412079_C_T"])[0]  # PheWAS — one variant × all traits t0 = time.perf_counter() z  = db.zscore_variant(v)   # reads 9 trait chunks; returns float32(T) print(f"PheWAS: {(time.perf_counter()-t0)*1000:.1f} ms")  # GWAS — all variants × one trait t  = db.trait_index(["ukb-b-19953"])[0] t0 = time.perf_counter() z  = db.zscore_trait(t)     # reads 187 variant chunks; returns float32(V) print(f"GWAS:   {(time.perf_counter()-t0)*1000:.1f} ms")

title: Query benchmarks
text: Use pre-built masks top-hits. Supplying exactly 5e-8 1e-5 tophits() hits fast path; threshold triggers full column scan. Batch association lookups. many (variant, trait) pairs spread across chunks, single associations() call efficient looping individual phewas() gwas() calls, chunk read per block. Narrow regions PheWAS. Runtime scales roughly number variants window. 100 kb locus typically contains ~6–10× fewer variants 1 Mb window, reducing runtime proportionally.

title: Getting started with pleiodbr
text: connection holds variant trait tables memory reads z-scores demand binary chunks.
code: library(pleiodbr) library(ggplot2) library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: 'dplyr' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union  db <- open_pleiodb("/local-scratch/data/pleiodb/main.pleiodb") db #> pleiodb database #>   path:           /local-scratch/data/pleiodb/main.pleiodb #>   format version: 3 #>   variants (V):   95,378 #>   traits (T):     4,159 #>   chunk shape:    512×512

title: Getting started with pleiodbr
code: pw_ann <- pw |>   left_join(db$traits[, c("trait_id", "trait_name")], by = "trait_id") |>   arrange(pval) |>   mutate(x = row_number(), log10p = -log10(pval), sig = pval < 5e-8)  top_labels <- pw_ann |> filter(pval < 1e-20)  ggplot(pw_ann, aes(x, log10p, colour = sig)) +   geom_point(size = 0.7, alpha = 0.6) +   geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(5e-8), linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +   ggrepel::geom_text_repel(data = top_labels, aes(label = trait_name),                            size = 2.5, max.overlaps = 15) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("FALSE" = "steelblue", "TRUE" = "firebrick"),                       guide = "none") +   labs(x = "Trait (ranked by p-value)", y = expression(-log[10](p)),        title = "PheWAS: 19:45412079_C_T (APOE region)") +   theme_bw(base_size = 11)

code: hits <- tophits(   db,   traits = c("ukb-b-19953", "ebi-a-GCST006867"),   pval   = 5e-8 ) hits |>   left_join(db$traits[, c("trait_id","trait_name")], by = "trait_id") |>   count(trait_name, wt = NULL) #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>   trait_name                n #>   <chr>                 <int> #> 1 Body mass index (BMI)  2077 #> 2 Type 2 diabetes         191

title: Getting started with pleiodbr
code: ldl_instruments <- c(   "19:45412079_C_T",  # APOE region   "1:55014160_C_T",   # PCSK9 region   "2:21870368_A_G"    # APOB region ) assoc <- associations(   db,   variants = ldl_instruments,   traits   = c("ebi-a-GCST90018961",   # LDL cholesterol                "ebi-a-GCST003116")     # Coronary artery disease ) assoc #> # A tibble: 6 × 9 #>   variant_id    trait_id       z     beta      se     pval    eaf      n imputed #>   <chr>         <chr>      <dbl>    <dbl>   <dbl>    <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl> <lgl>   #> 1 19:45412079_… ebi-a-G… -119.   -0.526   0.00441 0        0.0796 3.51e5 FALSE   #> 2 1:55014160_C… ebi-a-G…    1.98  0.0127  0.00639 4.77e- 2 0.725  6.14e4 FALSE   #> 3 2:21870368_A… ebi-a-G…    1.94  0.0141  0.00727 5.24e- 2 0.975  3.84e5 FALSE   #> 4 19:45412079_… ebi-a-G…   -6.5  -0.296   0.0455  8.03e-11 0.0796 3.30e3 FALSE   #> 5 1:55014160_C… ebi-a-G…   -0.38 -0.00966 0.0254  7.04e- 1 0.725  3.88e3 FALSE   #> 6 2:21870368_A… ebi-a-G…    0.61  0.0367  0.0602  5.42e- 1 0.975  5.62e3 FALSE

title: Getting started with pleiodbr
text: to_twosamplemr() converts pleiodbr result tibble directly format expected TwoSampleMR. , “ebi--GCST90013864”
code: exposure <- tophits(db, "ukb-b-19953") |>   to_twosamplemr("exposure", trait_name = "BMI")  outcome <- associations(db, exposure$SNP, "ebi-a-GCST90013864") |>   to_twosamplemr("outcome", trait_name = "Coronary artery disease")  dat <- TwoSampleMR::harmonise_data(exposure, outcome, action = 1) #> Harmonising BMI (ukb-b-19953) and Coronary artery disease (ebi-a-GCST90013864) res <- TwoSampleMR::mr(dat) #> Analysing 'ukb-b-19953' on 'ebi-a-GCST90013864' res[, c("method", "nsnp", "b", "se", "pval")] #>                      method nsnp          b          se          pval #> 1                  MR Egger 2073 0.11363427 0.016539197  8.426264e-12 #> 2           Weighted median 2073 0.12057780 0.006267515  1.761253e-82 #> 3 Inverse variance weighted 2073 0.13969146 0.005267603 5.855651e-155 #> 4               Simple mode 2073 0.09389754 0.036452118  1.006624e-02 #> 5             Weighted mode 2073 0.10590915 0.031809157  8.852135e-04

title: Getting started with pleiodbr
code: TwoSampleMR::mr_scatter_plot(res, dat) #> $`ukb-b-19953.ebi-a-GCST90013864`

title: Getting started with pleiodbr
code: library(tidyr)  traits_5 <- c(   "ukb-b-19953",        # BMI   "ebi-a-GCST006867",   # Type 2 diabetes   "ebi-a-GCST90018961", # LDL cholesterol   "ebi-a-GCST003116",   # Coronary artery disease   "ebi-a-GCST90002412"  # LDL (larger N) )  rho_long <- rho(db, traits_5, traits_5)  labels <- db$traits |>   filter(trait_id %in% traits_5) |>   mutate(label = paste0(sub(" \\(.*", "", trait_name), "\n(", trait_id, ")")) |>   select(trait_id, label)  rho_plot <- rho_long |>   left_join(labels, by = c("trait_id_1" = "trait_id")) |> rename(l1 = label) |>   left_join(labels, by = c("trait_id_2" = "trait_id")) |> rename(l2 = label)  ggplot(rho_plot, aes(l1, l2, fill = rho)) +   geom_tile(colour = "white") +   geom_text(aes(label = round(rho, 2)), size = 3) +   scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#4575B4", mid = "white", high = "#D73027",                        midpoint = 0, limits = c(-1, 1), name = "ρ") +   labs(x = NULL, y = NULL, title = "Phenotypic correlations") +   theme_bw(base_size = 9) +   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))

title: Getting started with pleiodbr
code: fto <- phewas(db, "16:53e6-54e6") |>   filter(trait_id == "ukb-b-19953") |>   mutate(pos_mb = as.numeric(sub(".*:(\\d+)_.*", "\\1", variant_id)) / 1e6)  ggplot(fto, aes(pos_mb, -log10(pval), colour = imputed, shape = imputed)) +   geom_point(size = 1.8, alpha = 0.85) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("FALSE" = "steelblue", "TRUE" = "#E07B39"),                       labels = c("Observed", "Imputed")) +   scale_shape_manual(values = c("FALSE" = 16, "TRUE" = 17),                      labels = c("Observed", "Imputed")) +   geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(5e-8), linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +   labs(x = "Position on chr16 (Mb)", y = expression(-log[10](p)),        colour = NULL, shape = NULL,        title = "FTO locus — BMI (chr16:53–54 Mb)") +   theme_bw(base_size = 11) +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: Authors and Citation
text: Gibran Hemani. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Hemani G (2026). pleiodbr: Read pleiodb GWAS Databases R. R package version 0.1.0, https://explodecomputer.github.io/pleiodbr/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {pleiodbr: Read pleiodb GWAS Databases from R},   author = {Gibran Hemani},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://explodecomputer.github.io/pleiodbr/}, }

title: pleiodbr
text: R reader pleiodb — binary database format storing GWAS z-scores across thousands traits genome-wide scale. pleiodbr reads .pleiodb databases directly R Python dependency. supports PheWAS, GWAS, top-hits, arbitrary variant×trait block queries, phenotypic correlation (rho) queries. queries return tidy tibbles.

title: pleiodbr
text: zstdlite dependency R-universe (yet CRAN). hit zstdlite installation error, install first: Dependencies: zstdlite, tibble, dplyr, jsonlite.
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("explodecomputer/pleiodbr") install.packages("zstdlite", repos = c("https://coolbutuseless.r-universe.dev",                                         "https://cloud.r-project.org"))

title: pleiodbr
code: library(pleiodbr)  # Open a database db <- open_pleiodb("/path/to/main.pleiodb") print(db) #> pleiodb database #>   path:           /path/to/main.pleiodb #>   format version: 3 #>   variants (V):   95,378 #>   traits (T):     4,159 #>   chunk shape:    512×512  # PheWAS — one variant across all traits phewas(db, "16:53800954_C_T")      # exact ALID phewas(db, "16:53e6-54e6")         # genomic region  # GWAS — all variants for one trait gwas(db, "ukb-b-19953")            # Body mass index  # Top hits tophits(db, traits = c("ukb-b-19953", "ebi-a-GCST006867"), pval = 5e-8)  # Arbitrary variant × trait block associations(   db,   variants = c("16:53800954_C_T", "19:45412079_C_T"),   traits   = c("ukb-b-19953", "ebi-a-GCST006867") )  # Phenotypic correlation rho(db, "ukb-b-19953", "ebi-a-GCST006867")

title: pleiodbr
text: query functions except rho() return tibble : rho() returns long-format tibble columns trait_id_1, trait_id_2, rho.

title: pleiodbr
text: .pleiodb directory stores V×T matrix GWAS z-scores (variants × traits) zstd-compressed 512×512 int16 chunks. also stores effective sample sizes (Neff), pairwise phenotypic correlations (rho), optional imputed z-scores produced LD reference completion. Python pleiodb package builds databases OpenGWAS VCF files.

title: pleiodbr
text: vignette("pleiodbr") — full walkthrough Manhattan plots, PheWAS plots, Mendelian randomisation example. vignette("benchmarks") — wall-clock timings every query type guide drives performance.

title: pleiodbr
text: Vignettes require live .pleiodb database. easiest way set environment via mamba:
code: mamba env create -f environment.yml mamba run -n pleiodbr Rscript setup.R mamba run -n pleiodbr Rscript -e "devtools::build_vignettes()"

title: Manhattan plot — manhattan_plot

title: Manhattan plot — manhattan_plot
text: dat tibble least variant_id, pval, optionally imputed (logical) columns. threshold Genome-wide significance threshold (default 5e-8). Drawn dashed horizontal line. suggestive Suggestive significance threshold (default 1e-5). Drawn dotted line. Set NULL omit. highlight_imputed Logical. Colour imputed variants distinct shade (default TRUE). effect dat imputed column. title Plot title string (default NULL = title).

title: Manhattan plot — manhattan_plot

title: Rho (phenotypic correlation) query — rho
text: db pleiodb object open_pleiodb(). traits_1 Character vector OpenGWAS trait IDs. traits_2 Character vector OpenGWAS trait IDs.

text: tibble columns trait_id_1, trait_id_2, rho covering cross-product pairs. Use tidyr::pivot_wider() matrix form.

what: Convert pleiodbr results to TwoSampleMR format — to_twosamplemr
title: Convert pleiodbr results to TwoSampleMR format — to_twosamplemr
text: Reformats tibble phewas(), gwas(), tophits(), associations() format expected TwoSampleMR::mr().

title: Convert pleiodbr results to TwoSampleMR format — to_twosamplemr
code: to_twosamplemr(dat, type = c("exposure", "outcome"), trait_name = NULL)

title: Convert pleiodbr results to TwoSampleMR format — to_twosamplemr
text: dat tibble returned pleiodbr query. type "exposure" "outcome". Controls column suffix set produced (beta.exposure/se.exposure/... vs beta.outcome/...). trait_name Optional character scalar. supplied, used exposure outcome label; otherwise trait_id used.

title: Convert pleiodbr results to TwoSampleMR format — to_twosamplemr
text: data frame TwoSampleMR format, ready pass TwoSampleMR::harmonise_data().

title: Convert pleiodbr results to TwoSampleMR format — to_twosamplemr
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ db  <- open_pleiodb("/path/to/main.pleiodb") exp <- gwas(db, "ukb-b-19953") |> to_twosamplemr("exposure") out <- gwas(db, "ebi-a-GCST006867") |> to_twosamplemr("outcome") dat <- TwoSampleMR::harmonise_data(exp, out) TwoSampleMR::mr(dat) } # }

title: Top hits query — tophits
text: Returns significant variant-trait associations p-value threshold specified set traits.

title: Top hits query — tophits
code: tophits(db, traits, pval = 5e-08)

title: Top hits query — tophits
text: db pleiodb object open_pleiodb(). traits Character vector OpenGWAS trait IDs. Required. pval P-value threshold (default 5e-8). database stores pre-built masks 5e-8 1e-5; thresholds trigger full-column scan.

title: Top hits query — tophits
